problem: Determine the sum of all prime numbers $p$ for which there exists no integer solution in $x$ to the congruence $3(6x+1)\equiv 4\pmod p$.

classified_questions.question: What is the given congruence and what are we looking for in terms of prime numbers?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we simplify the congruence to make it easier to solve?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What does it mean for an integer to be invertible modulo p? What does it tell us about the existence of a solution for x?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What are the prime factors of 18? Why are these prime moduli for which an x cannot exist?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we determine the sum of all prime numbers p for which there exists no integer solution for x?
classified_questions.label: b
